title: abutilon tehuantepecense
intro: Abutilon tehuantepecense is a plant species within the Malvaceae family, also known as the hollyhock tree, native to Mexico and Central America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant's extracts and leaves have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, offering benefits such as wound healing, fever reduction, and relief from respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Abutilon tehuantepecense can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of certain conditions.
